business.headline: G7 in talks to set up permanent body to oversee critical minerals supplyG7 countries are discussing a permanent secretariat to carry forward critical minerals initiatives beyond rotating presidencies. The talks lay bare differences over stockpiling and leadership as members try to reduce reliance on China.

Ethanol gets price protection, maize doesn't: What's going wrong?The government has set the MSP for maize at Rs 2,400 per quintal. However, in mandis, farmers are often forced to sell at much lower rates.

Cognizant eyes up to 15,000 job cuts, India likely to see biggest impactThe possible job cuts come at a time when many large IT firms are restructuring. Slower spending by clients, rising automation, and the growing use of AI are changing the way companies operate.

Zee files case against Nykaa for alleged misuse of songs, seeks $210,000 damagesZee Entertainment has filed a lawsuit in the Delhi High Court, seeking damages of $210,000. The company claims that Nykaa used several of its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels to promote products to its large online audience.

Sensex, Nifty close lower as record-low rupee, oil worries weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex fell 251.61 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,017.79, while the Nifty 50 declined 86.50 points, or 0.36%, to settle at 24,032.80.
Gratuity rises under new labour rules, but what if you quit before 5 years?New labour laws may boost your gratuity, but does quitting before five years mean missing out completely? Let's have a look.

2% DA hike explained: Only Rs 600 more on a Rs 30,000 basic pay?A 2% Dearness Allowance (DA) hike may sound like good news, but the actual increase in your salary is fairly modest. On a Rs 30,000 basic pay, it adds just Rs 600 a month, so what's behind this small hike?
Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw names niece Claire Mazumdar as successorClaire Mazumdar is currently the founder and CEO of Bicara Therapeutics, a company that was incubated by Biocon.

Sensex, Nifty down: Why is stock market falling today?Markets had opened weak and extended losses through the morning, indicating that the election-led optimism seen in the previous session was short-lived.

Sensex, Nifty bounced back in April. What can investors expect in May?After a broad-based April rally, can markets sustain momentum in May as earnings take centre stage, separating fundamentally strong companies from momentum-driven stocks while global risks continue to cap sharp upside?

Sensex, Nifty slip at open as oil spike and weak rupee unsettle marketsThe BSE Sensex was down 170.40 points, or 0.22%, at 77,099.00 at 9:35 am, while the Nifty 50 fell 65.85 points, or 0.27%, to 24,053.45 as of 9:35 am.

Why Warren Buffett says he doesn't understand today's markets any moreAce investor and former CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, Warren Buffett, said today's market is harder to read, with trading increasingly driven by short-term bets.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will market fall amid weak rupee, elevated oil prices?Markets are headed for a cautious start as weak global cues, elevated oil prices and a soft rupee weigh on sentiment. Election results and quarterly earnings may still shape intraday moves across stocks.

OnEMI Technology IPO crosses 5x subscription on Day 3. Check latest GMPThe allotment of shares is expected to be finalised on May 6, 2026. The company is likely to list on the NSE and BSE on May 8, 2026.
NSE introduces electronic gold receipts to digitise gold tradingEach EGR is backed by real gold, ensuring that investors still own physical value, just without the need to store it themselves.

Govt shields fuel buyers, rules out relief for oil PSUs amid crude spike: ReportThe government has ruled out compensating state-run fuel retailers while keeping retail petrol, diesel and LPG prices steady. The move shifts part of the cost to bulk and industrial users as inflation concerns stay in focus.

FIFA World Cup 2026 faces broadcast uncertainty in India, ChinaIn India, a Reliance-Disney joint venture has offered $20 million for 2026 World Cup broadcast rights, a fraction of FIFA's ask, which was not acceptable to soccer's global governing body, two sources told Reuters on Monday.

Sensex, Nifty rise on election boost, gains capped by weak rupeeMarkets closed higher on Monday after early gains drew support from state election trends and earnings optimism. The rally lost steam later as a weak rupee, firmer crude and profit booking kept investors cautious.

Are rising fuel costs and global risks reshaping the aviation sector?With fuel costs climbing and geopolitical risks increasing, the aviation sector is feeling the strain, but is this a short-term phase or a lasting shift?

Your mobile number is key to your finances. Here's the upside and riskIn India, the mobile number is becoming the key identifier across banking, payments, loans and insurance. That convenience is widening access, but it is also sharpening concerns around fraud, privacy and control.

entertainment.headline: Ranveer Singh ideal for Shaktimaan: Mukesh Khanna's co-actor Surendra Pal Actor Surendra Pal, Mukesh Khanna's co-star in Shaktimaan the TV series, said Ranveer Singh would be the ideal choice to star in a film version of the show. Pal urged Khanna to reconsider his decision about not casting the Dhurandhar star.

How Viggo Mortensen reacted to Jamie Dornan's Aragorn casting in The Hunt for Gollum Director Andy Serkis shared Viggo Mortensen's reaction to Jamie Dornan taking over as Aragorn in The Lord of the Rings: The Hunt for Gollum.

Kriti Sanon deserved National Award for Mimi, film changed my career: Sai Tamhankar Sai Tamhankar said Mimi marked a turning point in her career and changed how the industry saw her. She also praised Kriti Sanon's National Award win.

Keralam wrap: Akshay Kumar shares fun pic from set of film with Vidya, Raashii Akshay Kumar wrapped the Kerala schedule of Anees Bazmee's untitled film and shared a set picture with Vidya Balan and Raashii Khanna. The update underlines his reunion with Vidya and marks his return to Bazmee's direction after 15 years.

Veteran Tamil producer RB Choudary dies in car accident in Udaipur Veteran Tamil producer RB Choudary died in a car accident in Udaipur, his team confirmed. Choudary's body will be brought to Chennai tomorrow.

Gaurav Gera shares BTS options of his viral 'doodh soda' dialogue from Dhurandhar Gaurav Gera revealed how the popular Dhurandhar dialogue "Darling Darling, Dil Kyu Toda" was created. He shared multiple funny options suggested by Aditya Dhar.

Guess I called it: Pooja Hegde drops old video from Jana Nayagan set after Vijay win Actor Pooja Hegde shared a playful reel from Jana Nayagan set featuring Vijay, besides a congratulatory photo with the latter after his party TVK saw a landslide win in Tamil Nadu polls.

Is Alia Bhatt doing a 20-day shoot for Tumbbad 2? Here's what we know Alia Bhatt is reportedly set to shoot for Tumbbad 2 over 20 days for a key role in the sequel. Read to know details about her role.

Milind Soman swims from Europe to Africa; fans call him an absolute inspiration Milind Soman swam across the Strait of Gibraltar from Tarifa in Spain to the coast of Morocco on May 1. He shared the experience with fans on social media.

Amid engagement buzz, here's how Zoe Kravitz dodged ring reveal at Met Gala Actor Zoe Kravitz arrived at the Met Gala 2026 in a black outfit, keeping her left hand mostly out of view. Harry Styles skipped the event altogether. The move kept their engagement rumours alive, with neither star commenting on the speculation.
Shilpa Shirodkar recalls the 90s, shares old pics with Madhuri Dixit Shilpa Shirodkar shared nostalgic throwback photos from the 90s with Madhuri Dixit, taken during a live show in Indore.

Phalke, Baahubali to KJo: Raja Ravi Varma's legacy lives on in Indian cinema Karan Johar's Met Gala debut in a Manish Malhotra ensemble drew directly from Raja Ravi Varma's paintings. The tribute highlighted how the artist's imagery continues to shape Indian cinema, costume design and popular culture.

Christopher Nolan compares Homer to Marvel and DC comic book culture Christopher Nolan said that Homer's The Odyssey contained the original superheroes and called Homer the Marvel of its day. Describing Homer's place in storytelling, Nolan said he was "the sort of George Lucas of his time."

Reunion alert: Kareena Kapoor to star opposite Salman Khan in superhero film? Actor Kareena Kapoor Khan is reportedly in talks to star opposite Salman Khan in Raj and DK's untitled superhero film. If the deal is sealed, it will mark their first on-screen reunion since the 2015 hit, Bajrangi Bhaijaan.

The Last Tenant starring Irrfan Khan, Vidya Balan releases after 25 years The Last Tenant, starring Irrfan Khan and Vidya Balan, was released on YouTube on his sixth death anniversary after being unreleased for nearly 25 years.

METkaKing: Was Vijay Varma at Met Gala 2026? Here's the twist Actor Vijay Varma shared images of himself as Brij Bhatti from Matka King at the Met Gala. The playful 'METkaKing' post tapped into the event's online buzz.

Shekhar Suman says talent beats visibility, cites Akshaye Khanna in Dhurandhar Shekhar Suman said that talent and strong performances matter more than social media visibility in today's film industry. He cited Akshaye Khanna's impactful role in Dhurandhar as an example of how a powerful performance can redefine an actor's relevance.

Suriya's Karuppu gets censored ahead of May 14 release. Here are the details Suriya's action drama Karuppu has cleared the censor process ahead of its May 14 worldwide release. The update keeps the film on schedule as interest builds around Suriya and Trisha's reunion and its divine justice theme.

Shekhar Suman on losing Devdas, finding Heeramandi and never chasing work Actor Shekhar Suman recalled turning down the role of Chunni Babu in Devdas in an exclusive interview. He said working with filmmaker Sanjay Leela Bhansali in Heeramandi later made up for the regret and introduced him to younger viewers.

general news.headline: Congress extends support to Vijay's TVK for government formation in Tamil NaduCongress extends support to Vijay's TVK for government formation in Tamil Nadu, wants power sharing: Sources

Murder and absolute mayhem: No poriborton in Bengal post-poll violenceThe Poriborton wave in West Bengal might have delivered a landslide to the BJP, but on the ground, claims and reports of murder, violence, arson and vandalism directed towards Trinamool Congress's leaders and assets have surfaced. This tells the all-too-familiar story of post-poll violence in Bengal under the nose of thousands of CAPF personnel.

Mamata Banerjee refuses to resign. What happens next?Mamata Banerjee has refused to resign after the BJP's victory in Bengal, alleging the election was rigged, and the state was forcibly captured. With the government's term ending on May 6, can she hold onto her seat?

More mocked us than supported us: Vijay on TVK's historic winIn a historic upset, the TVK defeated the incumbent DMK and emerged as the single-largest party, winning 108 seats in the 234-member Tamil Nadu Assembly. The victory marked the first time since 1967 that a party outside the DMK–AIADMK duopoly is in a position to form a government.

In Assam and Bengal, Congress is standing on a very narrow Muslim baseThe Congress won 21 of the 390 seats it contested in Assam and West Bengal. Of the 21 winners, 20 are Muslims. The party, which attacks the BJP as communal, is getting caught in its own trap. And the warning came from within Congress way back in 2014.

India lucky to have Modi: Trump congratulates PM after historic Bengal winDonald Trump praises PM Narendra Modi after BJP's landmark Bengal win, calling it historic and decisive, while also expressing admiration for Modi's leadership in a message that adds global weight to the result.

Vijay's secret team behind TVK's Tamil Nadu winBehind the charisma of Thalapathy Vijay was a tightly built team that worked behind the scenes in making the TVK the single largest party in Tamil Nadu. The TVK managed to break the duopoly of the two Dravidian behemoths. Who is in Vijay's secret team behind TVK's Tamil Nadu win?

SIR impact: Where West Bengal's voter rolls shrank most, TMC held firmIn the months leading up to West Bengal's Assembly polls, the Election Commission of India removed roughly one in 10 voters from the state's electoral rolls.

The Met wrap: Naked dresses, million-dollar Indian jewels and steel coutureFrom "steel"-worthy couture to naked dresses and bold, art-inspired sartorial interpretations, here's the only Met Gala red carpet recap you need to see.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Why Iran is striking UAE amid ceasefireThe UAE's status as a regional economic powerhouse, coupled with its close strategic ties to the United States and Israel, has made it a prime target of Iran, leaving the Gulf's most globally connected hub among the hardest hit by Iranian strikes.

8th Pay Commission update: Where things stand after 6 monthsSix months since its formation, the 8th Pay Commission is showing signs of progress, but key recommendations, timelines and clarity on pay revisions for central government employees are still awaited.

Vijay's oath likely on May 7 after TVK's blockbuster Tamil Nadu debutAccording to sources, the actor-politician is exploring talks with the Congress, Left parties, and other smaller regional outfits, including the Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi (VCK), CPI(M), CPI, and the Indian Union Muslim League (IUML), to secure numbers for a coalition arrangement. Invitations to these parties are expected to be sent soon.

IndiGo flight evacuated in Chandigarh after passenger's power bank catches fireThe evacuation was ordered after a passenger alerted the flight's crew about a fire in his power bank, ostensibly from an internal explosion. The flight, carrying 198 passengers and six crew members, had already landed by the time the alarm was raised.

Annamalai: The missing star of Tamil Nadu electionOnce projected as the BJP's face of expansion in Tamil Nadu, IPS officer-turned-politician K Annamalai found himself off the ballot as the party pivoted back to alliance with the AIADMK. What does his absence from the centre stage highlight? How does his absence reflect the BJP's own dilemma in Tamil Nadu?

Live: All 5 Congress MLA-elects ready to ally with Vijay's TVK, say sourcesAssembly Election Result Live Updates: The 2026 Assembly results are redrawing India's political map, with the BJP poised to form its first government in West Bengal after crossing the 200-mark in a 294-seat House, while retaining Assam for a third straight term. In Bengal, counting is in its final leg, with the Trinamool Congress leading in the lone remaining seat — Rajarhat New Town — where one more round is pending. The state voted in two phases on April 23 and 29, recording a turnout of over 92 per cent. The Election Commission has also ordered a repoll in Falta on May 21, citing "severe electoral offences," with results to follow separately. Beyond Bengal, the national picture is crystallising — most notably in Tamil Nadu, where actor-politician Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) is set to form the government in its debut outing, signalling a major political shift. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.

No question of my resignation, haven't lost election: Mamata Banerjee defiantMamata Banerjee rejects calls to step down after defeat, alleges large-scale voter manipulation and violence, and signals a renewed political fight even as INDIA bloc leaders rally behind her.

UP ends prepaid smart meters, shifts all power connections back to postpaidUttar Pradesh government has scrapped mandatory prepaid smart meters and reverted all power connections to postpaid billing following consumer complaints.

Trapped with a virus: How an Antarctic cruise turned into horror at seaThe WHO said all affected passengers fell ill between April 6 and 28, with symptoms that rapidly progressed to pneumonia and acute respiratory distress.

US to arm Iranian citizens? Trump's plan to reignite anti-regime fightDonald Trump said an unarmed population has little chance against a heavily armed state, pointing to the high death toll among anti-regime demonstrators.
Gratuity rises under new labour rules, but what if you quit before 5 years?New labour laws may boost your gratuity, but does quitting before five years mean missing out completely? Let's have a look.

TVK plans talks with Congress, Left parties to form coalition govt: SourcesVijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), which won 108 seats in a strong debut in Tamil Nadu, but fell short of a majority, is eyeing talks with the Congress, Left and smaller parties to form a coalition government, sources told India Today TV.

Ranveer Singh ideal for Shaktimaan: Mukesh Khanna's co-actor Surendra PalActor Surendra Pal, Mukesh Khanna's co-star in Shaktimaan the TV series, said Ranveer Singh would be the ideal choice to star in a film version of the show. Pal urged Khanna to reconsider his decision about not casting the Dhurandhar star.

How Viggo Mortensen reacted to Jamie Dornan's Aragorn casting in The Hunt for GollumDirector Andy Serkis shared Viggo Mortensen's reaction to Jamie Dornan taking over as Aragorn in The Lord of the Rings: The Hunt for Gollum.

sports.headline: Sanju Samson powers CSK's double over Delhi to add spice to Chennai numbers game IPL 2026, DC vs CSK: Sanju Samson struck an unbeaten 87 to help Chennai Super Kings successfully chase 156 in Delhi on Tuesday and complete a double over the Capitals. With 10 points from as many matches, the Super Kings have joined the season of numbers game in Chennai. 118 for TVK and a possible 16 for the Super Kings is all many down South will be hoping for.

Vinesh Phogat lands in trouble: Wrestler flagged for missing dope test Vinesh Phogat has been handed her first whereabouts failure by the International Testing Agency after missing an out-of-competition dope test in December 2025. While not an anti-doping violation, the warning puts her one step closer to potential sanctions under global testing rules.

Pegged back by back-to-back losses, Punjab Kings hunt momentum vs SRH After staying unbeaten for more than half of the IPL 2026, Punjab Kings have lost back to back matches. As they take on SRH in their 10th game of the season, assistant coach Brad Haddin hopes that Punjab find much-needed momentum going into the playoffs stage.

IPL tickets scam busted by Hyderabad Police after SRH vs KKR game Hyderabad police arrested three men for black-marketing complimentary IPL tickets during the SRH-KKR match at Uppal. Investigators said forged VIP letterheads were used to obtain passes, exposing a racket around match access.

DC vs CSK: Fit-again Lungi Ngidi returns after mandatory concussion layoff IPL 2026, DC vs CSK: Lungi Ngidi has returned to the Delhi Capitals playing XI after completing his mandatory concussion lay-off, providing a timely boost to the team's pace attack ahead of the clash against Chennai Super Kings.

Does BCCI control ICC? South Africa spinner makes massive allegation Simon Harmer said India's financial muscle gives the BCCI decisive sway over cricket's direction. His remarks came as South Africa's recent rise challenged old hierarchies on and off the field.

Thomas Cup bronze, no buzz: Chirag, Prannoy seek more recognition Chirag Shetty and HS Prannoy said India's Thomas Cup bronze returned with little recognition at home. They said the muted response makes it harder for players to stay motivated for team commitments.

Riyan Parag's elevation as Rajasthan captain 'makes no sense': Sanjay Manjrekar Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar has strongly criticised Rajasthan Royals for appointing Riyan Parag as the franchise's full-time captain for IPL 2026, saying the decision "makes no sense" from a cricketing perspective.

Bengaluru unlikely to host IPL final over MLA ticket issue IPL 2026: BCCI is reconsidering Bengaluru as host for the IPL 2026 play-offs and final amid a political row over MLA tickets. The uncertainty revives questions over venue management as RCB remain firmly in contention.

Ex-India cricketer quits TMC day after BJP sweep, slams outgoing sports minister Bengal's junior sports minister Manoj Tiwary has quit TMC just day after BJP's sweep in Bengal elections. Tiwary levels serious allegations against outgoing sports minister Aroop Biswas.

Arsenal shift focus from Premier League race to Atletico clash in UCL semi-finalArsenal and Atletico Madrid head into their Champions League semi-final second leg at the Emirates after a 1-1 draw in Madrid and reports of fireworks outside Atletico's London hotel. The tie pits Arsenal's control and defensive structure against Atletico's disruptive style, with the result to be decided over 90 minutes.

Virat Kohli told me I'll fit perfectly: Karthik opens up about RCB coaching roleDinesh Karthik said Virat Kohli's backing helped him accept Royal Challengers Bengaluru's batting coach and mentor role after retirement. The move strengthened RCB's support staff and preceded their IPL title.

MI need to look after Bumrah: Ravi Shastri decodes pacer's poor IPL 2026 formFormer India coach Ravi Shastri blamed mental fatigue for Jasprit Bumrah's poor IPL 2026 form, saying the Mumbai Indians must carefully manage the workload of their pace spearhead.

Relief from Indian captaincy lifted Rohit Sharma's batting: Gavaskar after MI beat LSGIPL 2026: Sunil Gavaskar said Rohit Sharma's batting has grown since the captaincy burden was lifted. The remark came after Rohit's 84 off 44 led Mumbai Indians past Lucknow Super Giants.

I was never rated as a T20 player: Rahul glad to silence IPL strike-rate chatterKL Rahul admitted that things have not always gone his way in T20 cricket, but said he is proud to have broken the perception of being only a Test specialist after years of criticism over his strike rate and white-ball game.

No one really cares: Sat-Chi's viral cryptic post after Thomas Cup medal winSatwik Rankirajreddy and Chirag Shetty shared a cryptic Instagram story after India's Thomas Cup bronze. The post drew attention to the muted reception around the team's campaign and return home.

Gold medal in hand, no way home: Pole vault prodigy Kavinraja left strandedPole vaulter Kavinraja S was stranded at Rajahmundry railway station for over 12 hours while returning from Bhubaneswar with his poles. The episode highlighted the travel difficulties athletes face while carrying specialised equipment.

Ollie Robinson sets sights on England Test recall after positive McCullum feedbackEngland pacer Ollie Robinson said encouraging messages from head coach Brendon McCullum and managing director Rob Key have revived his hopes of returning to England's Test side.

Aimless Rishabh Pant had no clear-cut plans for LSG during MI loss: Shaun PollockShaun Pollock questioned Rishabh Pant's handling of Lucknow Super Giants' bowlers after the loss to Mumbai Indians. The criticism sharpened focus on LSG's fading playoff hopes and Pant's difficult season.

How MS Dhoni's World Cup-winning six inspired Raghu Sharma's IPL journeyMumbai Indians leg-spinner Raghu Sharma said watching MS Dhoni hit the World Cup-winning six against Sri Lanka at the Wankhede Stadium in 2011 inspired him to take cricket seriously despite weighing 102 kilograms and facing doubts over his future in the sport.

Who will telecast FIFA World Cup in India after Reliance-Disney deal collapse?FIFA has not finalised a broadcaster for the 2026 World Cup in India after rejecting the Reliance-Disney joint venture's bid. The standoff has left one of football's biggest markets awaiting a late rights deal weeks before kick-off.

DC batting overly reliant on KL Rahul: Ex-India star ahead of CSK clashIPL 2026: Aakash Chopra has said Delhi Capitals are relying too heavily on KL Rahul ahead of their match against Chennai Super Kings. The concern has sharpened as Delhi's brittle batting returns and painful home defeats leave little margin for error.

You should go home: How Ruturaj sparked Overton-Tilak clash during MI vs CSKJamie Overton said Ruturaj Gaikwad's teasing on the previous ball fed the brief on-field exchange with Tilak Varma during MI vs CSK. Ruturaj revealed what he told to Overton, which led to the spat taking place.

What's wrong with Bumrah? Sunil Gavaskar explains MI pacer's IPL 2026 issuesSunil Gavaskar has said Jasprit Bumrah is overcomplicating his bowling during a lean IPL 2026 for Mumbai Indians. With Sanjay Bangar suggesting a short break, MI must decide how to restore his rhythm.

Foul play in RR sale? Kal Somani-led group say they were pushed out of dealThe Kal Somani-led consortium has claimed it was pushed out of the Rajasthan Royals ownership deal despite leading the bid for six months. The statement challenges reports that it withdrew and raises questions over how the franchise sale was handled.

Release Rishabh Pant as captain, we're losing him as batter: Pundits tell LSGRohan Gavaskar and Manoj Tiwary have urged Lucknow Super Giants to remove Rishabh Pant as captain after the defeat to Mumbai Indians. They said the burden of leadership is affecting his batting and LSG should let him play only as a batter.

When will Hardik Pandya return from injury for MI in IPL 2026?IPL 2026: Hardik Pandya missed Mumbai Indians' match against Lucknow Super Giants with a back spasm. His uncertain return leaves MI monitoring their captain closely during a crucial stage of IPL 2026.

Neymar accused of assault on Santos teammate after heated training clashIPL 2026: Santos are investigating an alleged training-ground altercation between Neymar and Robinho Jr. The episode has raised player-safety concerns and put the youngster's future at the club in focus.

Rishabh Pant's Rs 27 crore price tag under scrutiny? Langer defends LSG captainIPL 2026: Justin Langer defended Rishabh Pant's form and tactical calls after Lucknow Super Giants lost to Mumbai Indians. The support came as Pant's modest returns and LSG's inability to sustain strong positions stayed under focus.

No T20 talk or life-changing advice: How Rohit formulated Rickelton's MI riseRyan Rickleton said his discussions with Rohit Sharma are focused on batting, not only T20 cricket. He said Rohit's calm, clarity, and chemistry at the top are helping the Mumbai Indians stay alive in the playoff race.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
